channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Self-growth of programmable 2D- and 3D-coupling responsive coating for anti-counterfeiting
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 23 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67742-0

Programable coatings are promising for information encryption, though it is challenging to design stimuli-responsive materials. Here the authors integrate a photochromic switch into a polymer system for programmable encryption.
item.dc:title: Self-growth of programmable 2D- and 3D-coupling responsive coating for anti-counterfeiting
item.dc:creator: Hong Wang
Haitao Deng
Junyi Chen
Yong Tian
Fuhua Huang
Yi Zhao
Zhihua Zhou
Jiaxi Cui
Jian Chen
Xudong Chen

item.title: All-optical temporal integration mediated by subwavelength heat antennas
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 23 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67726-0

The authors demonstrate the use of standing waves to generate localised heat using nanoantennae in integrated photonics. They utilise this heat to mediate all-photonic integration of 50 GHz optical signals, showing experimentally, the potential for achieving programmable nonlinear activation functions. This is a step towards full optical encoding in integrated photonic processors.
item.dc:title: All-optical temporal integration mediated by subwavelength heat antennas
item.dc:creator: Yi Zhang
Nikolaos Farmakidis
Ioannis Roumpos
Miltiadis Moralis-Pegios
Apostolos Tsakyridis
June Sang Lee
Bowei Dong
Yuhan He
Samarth Aggarwal
Nikos Pleros
Harish Bhaskaran
